headline: Vanguard’s MGC Has Returned 297% Over 10 Years, But Is It Really Conservative?
summary: The word “conservative” gets attached to a lot of funds that don’t quite earn it. Vanguard Mega Cap Index Fund ETF Shares (NYSEARCA:MGC) holds roughly 230 of the largest US companies, charges just 5 basis points annually, and has been around since December 2007. headline: Big Tech Stocks Rout Is Flashing Signals of a Turnaround
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The wreckage in large technology stocks that sent the Nasdaq 100 Index into a correction is flashing signs that have marked turning points for the group in the past.Chief among the signals is how much of Big Tech’s valuation premium to the rest of the market has been erased. Such a compression has historically set the sector up for outperformance.After an 11% slide from its last record in October, the tech-focused gauge now trades at 21 times projected 12-month earnings, just 1.7

headline: Apple’s New AI Hire Puts Focus On Siri And Investor Expectations
summary: Apple has hired former Google executive Lilian Rincon as vice president of AI product marketing. Rincon previously led work on Google Assistant, bringing direct experience in consumer AI assistants. The appointment comes ahead of expected Siri upgrades and Apple’s 50th anniversary. The move aligns with anticipated AI announcements at Apple’s June Worldwide Developers Conference. For investors watching NasdaqGS:AAPL, the hire lands at a time when Apple’s AI story is front and center. The...

headline: TSMC 2nm Ramp And AI Demand Reframe Growth And Valuation Narrative
summary: TSMC (NYSE:TSM) has started production of its next generation 2nm chips, marking a new step in advanced semiconductor manufacturing. The move comes alongside record demand for AI chips from major customers such as NVIDIA and Apple. The company has raised its long term growth outlook as AI related orders and 2nm capacity ramp shape its business mix. TSMC is the leading pure play foundry serving global chip designers, and its 2nm technology is set to sit at the core of future AI, data center...

headline: Goldman initiates Qualcomm at Neutral as smartphone weakness offsets tailwinds
summary: Investing.com -- Goldman Sachs has begun coverage of Qualcomm with a Neutral rating and a 12-month price target of $135, saying the chipmaker’s push into automotive and industrial markets is being offset by headwinds in its core smartphone business, leaving the stock with limited near-term upside.
